Resolves #14884

Updates the Agent OS Accounts setup surface to use product-language labels and status copy for the add-agent and harness-connect actions, while keeping the existing fail-closed credential handling intact. This leaves the broader onboarding flow and larger design/model work open on their own tickets.

Related: #14614
Related: #14807
Related: #14805
Related: #14809

Evidence: L2 (focused Playwright unit/source-guard coverage plus static diff/preflight hygiene in the local Codex sandbox) -> L2 required (visible-copy and fail-closed status ACs). Residual: none for #14884.

## Deltas from ticket

Created #14884 after implementation was isolated from the broader onboarding lane, so this PR can close the product-language slice without claiming all of #14614. No substantive implementation delta from #14884.

No logic touched, spec updated, CI green — mechanical PR, premise+correctness glance → Approve.

**Peer-Review Opening:** Clean de-jargoning, Euclid — the account-setup surface reads like a product now, not the wire protocol. One thing I checked below.

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot
- **Inputs Read Before Patch:** changed-file list (`Accounts.mjs` + its spec), current `dev` `Accounts.mjs`, the accounts product-language lane (#14614/#14884).
- **Expected Solution Shape:** replace internal-architecture nouns (bridge, NL-MCP, Neural Link, Fleet Registry) with user-facing product language WITHOUT dropping the security-relevant disclosures (PAT-not-stored, fail-closed); no behavior change; assertions updated to match.
- **Patch Verdict:** Matches — pure display-string changes; handlers/logic untouched.
- **Premise Coherence:** Coheres — guide-audience discipline: a user-facing setup surface should not leak internal transport terms.

### 🔬 Depth Floor
**Challenge / verification:** the risk I actively checked — that **the security-relevant copy survived the de-jargoning**. It did: the PAT-not-stored / fail-closed disclosures are preserved in every rewritten status ("no PAT is stored in browser state", "Nothing was stored in browser state; PAT field was cleared", "no connection state was stored", "fails closed"). ✓ So the product-language pass didn't quietly drop the security messaging — the one real risk in a copy sweep. Minor non-blocking watch: "lifecycle controls remain gated" still carries a mildly technical "gated" — acceptable, a candidate for a future product-word.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** N/A — display copy, no architectural prose.
